title: Blend A Survival-Style Sidecar With Rank Averaging Instead Of Probability Averaging
summary: AUC evaluates ranking, and ROC/AUC is invariant to monotone score transforms, so diverse models often combine better via rank blending than raw-probability averaging.
rationale: AUC evaluates ranking, and ROC/AUC is invariant to monotone score transforms, so diverse models often combine better via rank blending than raw-probability averaging. A discrete-time survival or random survival forest sidecar can learn event-time structure differently from the current pointwise tree stack.
implementation_hint: Train one sidecar on person-period style sequential features, using either a discrete-time hazard learner or random survival forest/landmark-style model, then ensemble it with the best pointwise GBDT by fold-wise rank averaging or a tiny leak-safe stacker on OOF predictions. If calibration is needed for stacking stability, prefer beta calibration over isotonic on small OOF sets.
expected_effect: May add incremental AUC through model diversity even if the sidecar is not individually best, especially on edge cases near stop/no-stop boundaries.
risk: Survival sidecars can be cumbersome to validate on this non-censored setup, and rank blending only helps if model errors are meaningfully decorrelated.
